description: Use these categories to code every deal's primary reason consistently. Pick the single best-fit primary category per deal; a secondary category is optional.

categories.label: Price / budget
categories.notes: Deal lost or won primarily on cost, TCO, or budget fit.

categories.label: Feature / capability gap
categories.notes: A specific required capability was missing, weaker, or not yet released.

categories.label: Existing relationship / incumbency
categories.notes: Competitor (or us) was already embedded/trusted; switching cost or loyalty was decisive.

categories.label: Timing / urgency mismatch
categories.notes: Deal stalled or lost due to buyer timeline or budget cycle — not a head-to-head loss.

categories.label: Support / implementation / service quality
categories.notes: Perceived or experienced difference in onboarding, support, or professional services.

categories.label: Brand / trust / risk perception
categories.notes: Buyer perception of vendor stability, market presence, analyst standing, or security posture.

categories.label: Sales process / rep execution
categories.notes: Outcome driven by how the deal was run rather than product/price.

categories.label: Overall product-market fit for this buyer
categories.notes: No single dealbreaker feature, but overall approach/architecture didn't fit.

categories.label: Other / unknown
categories.notes: Use when the reason genuinely isn't captured well by other categories, or wasn't documented.
